# Cloudflare Fundamentals

Learn about using Cloudflare and features that span across Cloudflare products

> Links below point directly to Markdown versions of each page. Any page can also be retrieved as Markdown by sending an `Accept: text/markdown` header to the page's URL without the `index.md` suffix (for example, `curl -H "Accept: text/markdown" https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/`).
>
> For other Cloudflare products, see the [Cloudflare documentation directory](https://developers.cloudflare.com/llms.txt).

## Overview

- [Cloudflare Fundamentals](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/index.md): Understand core Cloudflare concepts, set up your account, manage domains, and configure essential platform settings.

## Get started

- [Get started](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/get-started/index.md): Create a Cloudflare account, set up member permissions, and explore the Build, Protect & Connect, and Network services available to you.

## Organizations

- [Organizations](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/organizations/index.md): Manage multiple Cloudflare accounts from a single organization with centralized access control and audit logs.
- [Limitations](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/organizations/limitations/index.md): Review the current limitations of Cloudflare Organizations during the public beta.
- [Manage members](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/organizations/manage-members/index.md): Invite and manage members within a Cloudflare Organization, including assigning Super Administrator access.
- [Manage organizations](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/organizations/manage-organization/index.md): Rename your Cloudflare Organization, edit customer identification data, and manage settings with Terraform.
- [Set up](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/organizations/setup/index.md): Create a Cloudflare Organization, assign accounts, and invite members to manage resources centrally.

## Members and permissions

- [Members and permissions](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-members/index.md): Add members to your Cloudflare account and assign roles, scopes, and policies to control their access.
- [Set up dashboard SSO](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-members/dashboard-sso/index.md): Configure single sign-on (SSO) for the Cloudflare dashboard using your identity provider to enforce authenticated access for your email domain.
- [Manage](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-members/manage/index.md): Add, edit, and remove Cloudflare account members and their permission policies.
- [Policies](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-members/policies/index.md): Understand how Cloudflare account member policies combine actors, roles, and scopes to define access permissions.
- [Roles](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-members/roles/index.md): Review the account-scoped, domain-scoped, and resource-scoped roles available for Cloudflare account members.
- [Role scopes](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-members/scope/index.md): Choose account, domain, or resource-level scopes to control where Cloudflare member permissions apply.
- [User Groups](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-members/user-groups/index.md): Create and manage Cloudflare User Groups to assign shared permission policies to multiple account members.

## User profiles

- [User profiles](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/user-profiles/index.md): Manage your Cloudflare user profile, including login, 2FA, email, password, and account settings.
- [Two-factor authentication](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/user-profiles/2fa/index.md): Set up and manage two-factor authentication on your Cloudflare account using security keys, TOTP apps, or email.
- [Account recovery](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/user-profiles/account-recovery/index.md): Regain access to your Cloudflare account when you have lost your 2FA device and backup codes.
- [Email address and password](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/user-profiles/change-password-or-email/index.md): Learn how to change your email address or password associated with your account.
- [Profile settings](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/user-profiles/customize-account/index.md): Customize your Cloudflare dashboard language, appearance, timezone, and notification preferences.
- [Delete your Cloudflare account](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/user-profiles/delete-account/index.md): Permanently delete your Cloudflare user profile and remove all associated domains and billing information.
- [Log in to Cloudflare](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/user-profiles/login/index.md): Sign in to the Cloudflare dashboard using email and password, SSO, or social login with Apple, Google, or GitHub.
- [Multi-Factor Email Authentication](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/user-profiles/multi-factor-email-authentication/index.md): Cloudflare's Multi-Factor Email Authentication prevents unauthorized access by sending one-time codes to your email.
- [Verify email address](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/user-profiles/verify-email-address/index.md): Confirm the email address on your Cloudflare account to unlock dashboard features like adding members and updating preferences.

## Domains

- [Domains](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-domains/index.md): Add, configure, and manage your domains (zones) on Cloudflare, including DNS setup, subdomains, and SSL certificates.
- [Add multiple sites via automation](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-domains/add-multiple-sites-automation/index.md): To add multiple sites to Cloudflare at once and more efficiently, you can do so via the Cloudflare API.
- [Onboard a domain](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-domains/add-site/index.md): Learn how to onboard your domain to Cloudflare, to speed up and protect your website or application.
- [Change your domain version](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-domains/domain-version/index.md): Version Management allows you to safely test, deploy, and roll back changes to your zone configurations. By default, Version Management is not enabled on a zone.
- [Manage subdomains](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-domains/manage-subdomains/index.md): Create subdomains, set up redirects between subdomains and apex domains, and configure SSL/TLS for subdomains on Cloudflare.
- [Move a domain between Cloudflare accounts](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-domains/move-domain/index.md): Learn how to transfer a domain between Cloudflare accounts, including requirements, DNS settings, and SSL/TLS certificate management for seamless migration.
- [Pause Cloudflare](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-domains/pause-cloudflare/index.md): Temporarily pause Cloudflare on your domain to send traffic directly to your origin server for troubleshooting.
- [Redirect one domain to another](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-domains/redirect-domain/index.md): Set up domain redirects in Cloudflare to forward traffic from an alias domain to your primary domain using DNS records and redirect rules.
- [Remove a domain](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-domains/remove-domain/index.md): Remove a domain from your Cloudflare account, including required steps for DNS, subscriptions, and registrar settings.
- [Star domains](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-domains/star-zones/index.md): Star up to ten frequently used domains per account in the Cloudflare dashboard for quick access.

## account

- [Add abuse contact](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/account/account-security/abuse-contact/index.md): Set an abuse contact email address on your Cloudflare account to receive communications about potential abuse on your websites.
- [Audit Logs - version 2](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/account/account-security/audit-logs/index.md): Use Cloudflare Audit Logs v2 to track user-initiated and system-initiated actions across your account via the dashboard, API, or Logpush.
- [Allow Cloudflare access](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/account/account-security/cloudflare-access/index.md): Grant your Cloudflare Account Team temporary editing access to your account for migrations or sensitive configuration changes.
- [Set up SSO](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/manage-members/dashboard-sso/index.md): Configure single sign-on (SSO) for your Cloudflare dashboard to centralize authentication through your identity provider.
- [Leaked Password Notifications](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/account/account-security/leaked-password-notifications/index.md): Cloudflare checks your password against known data breaches at login and prompts you to reset it if a match is found.
- [Manage active sessions](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/account/account-security/manage-active-sessions/index.md): View and revoke active login sessions associated with your Cloudflare account to maintain account security.
- [Review audit logs - v1](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/account/account-security/review-audit-logs/index.md): Review Cloudflare Audit Logs v1 to track account activity through the dashboard or API.
- [SCIM provisioning](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/account/account-security/scim-setup/index.md): Provision users and groups into your Cloudflare account using the SCIM protocol with Okta, Microsoft Entra, or Authentik.
- [Provision with Authentik](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/account/account-security/scim-setup/authentik/index.md): Configure Authentik as a SCIM identity provider to provision users and groups into your Cloudflare account.
- [Provision with Microsoft Entra](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/account/account-security/scim-setup/entra/index.md): Configure Microsoft Entra as a SCIM identity provider to provision users and groups into your Cloudflare account.
- [Provision with Okta](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/account/account-security/scim-setup/okta/index.md): Configure Okta as a SCIM identity provider to provision users and groups into your Cloudflare account.
- [SCIM troubleshooting](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/account/account-security/scim-setup/troubleshooting/index.md): Restore Super Administrator access and resolve common SCIM provisioning issues on your Cloudflare account.
- [Secure compromised account](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/account/account-security/secure-a-compromised-account/index.md): If you observe suspicious activity within your Cloudflare account, secure your account with these steps.
- [Zone holds](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/account/account-security/zone-holds/index.md): Prevent other teams from adding your active Cloudflare zones to a different account by enabling zone holds.
- [Change Super Administrator](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/account/change-super-admin/index.md): Add or replace a Super Administrator on your Cloudflare account when someone leaves or loses access.
- [Create account](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/account/create-account/index.md): Learn how to create a new Cloudflare account.
- [Find account and zone IDs](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/account/find-account-and-zone-ids/index.md): Locate your Cloudflare account ID and zone ID in the dashboard for use with the API.

## api

- [Account API tokens](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/get-started/account-owned-tokens/index.md): Learn what account API tokens are, when to use them, and what they currently work with
- [Get Origin CA keys](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/get-started/ca-keys/index.md): Retrieve or change your Cloudflare Origin CA key used to authenticate Origin CA certificate API requests.
- [Create API token](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/get-started/create-token/index.md): Learn how to create a token to perform actions using the Cloudflare API.
- [Get Global API key (legacy)](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/get-started/keys/index.md): Retrieve or change your Cloudflare Global API key, a legacy authentication method with full account access.
- [Token formats](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/get-started/token-formats/index.md): Scannable API credential formats and leaked token detection.
- [API token template URLs](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/how-to/account-owned-token-template/index.md): Generate Cloudflare API tokens with pre-configured permissions using template URLs. Learn how to create and customize template URLs for any use case.
- [Control API Access](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/how-to/control-api-access/index.md): Restrict Cloudflare API access at the account or member level using Enterprise account controls.
- [Create tokens via API](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/how-to/create-via-api/index.md): Learn how to create API tokens via Cloudflare's API. Follow steps to define access policies, set restrictions, and generate tokens securely.
- [Make API calls](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/how-to/make-api-calls/index.md): Learn how to make API calls using Cloudflare's API with step-by-step instructions for Windows, including using curl and PowerShell, and handling JSON.
- [Restrict tokens](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/how-to/restrict-tokens/index.md): Limit Cloudflare API token usage by client IP address filtering and time-to-live (TTL) constraints.
- [Roll tokens](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/how-to/roll-token/index.md): Regenerate Cloudflare API token secrets to rotate credentials and maintain account security.
- [API deprecations](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/reference/deprecations/index.md): Track Cloudflare API deprecations, removal timelines, and replacement endpoints.
- [GraphQL API](https://developers.cloudflare.com/analytics/graphql-api/index.md): Query Cloudflare analytics and account data using the GraphQL API.
- [Rate limits](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/reference/limits/index.md): Understand Cloudflare API rate limits, rate-limiting headers, and how to handle throttled requests.
- [API token permissions](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/reference/permissions/index.md): Review available Cloudflare API token permissions for user, account, and zone resources.
- [REST API](https://developers.cloudflare.com/api/index.md): Access the Cloudflare REST API schema documentation for all available endpoints.
- [SDKs](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/reference/sdks/index.md): Use Cloudflare API SDKs for Go, TypeScript, and Python to integrate Cloudflare services into your applications.
- [API token templates](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/reference/template/index.md): Explore Cloudflare's API token templates to efficiently manage permissions. Start with a template and customize token permissions and resources as needed.
- [Wrangler API](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/wrangler/api/index.md): Use the Wrangler API to programmatically manage Cloudflare Workers and resources.
- [Troubleshooting](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/api/troubleshooting/index.md): Resolve common Cloudflare API token issues including verification failures, incorrect permissions, and syntax errors.

## concepts

- [Accounts, zones, and profiles](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/concepts/accounts-and-zones/index.md): Understand how Cloudflare organizes resources into user profiles, accounts, and zones, and where settings and products apply.
- [Cloudflare IP addresses](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/concepts/cloudflare-ip-addresses/index.md): Allow Cloudflare IP addresses at your origin server and configure your firewall to prevent accidental blocking of proxied traffic.
- [How Cloudflare DNS works](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/concepts/how-cloudflare-works/index.md): Understand how Cloudflare acts as your authoritative DNS provider and reverse proxy to optimize, cache, and protect your web traffic.
- [Traffic flow through Cloudflare](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/concepts/traffic-flow-cloudflare/index.md): Understand how public and private traffic on-ramps to and off-ramps from Cloudflare's global network for security and performance.

## new-features

- [Available RSS Feeds](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/new-features/available-rss-feeds/index.md): Read about the various RSS feeds available for Cloudflare's changelogs.
- [Consuming RSS Feeds](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/new-features/consuming-rss-feeds/index.md): Learn how to consume our changelog RSS feeds.

## performance

- [Improve SEO](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/performance/improve-seo/index.md): Use Cloudflare features like caching, HTTPS, and Crawler Hints to improve your website's search engine rankings.
- [Maintenance mode](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/performance/maintenance-mode/index.md): Make your website temporarily unavailable during large changes using Cloudflare Workers, Waiting Room, or Access.
- [Minimize downtime](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/performance/minimize-downtime/index.md): Learn how to minimize downtime while onboarding your domain onto Cloudflare.
- [Optimize site speed](https://developers.cloudflare.com/speed/index.md): Optimize your website performance using Cloudflare Speed features.
- [Prepare for surges or spikes in web traffic](https://developers.cloudflare.com/learning-paths/surge-readiness/concepts/index.md): Plan for traffic surges using the Cloudflare surge readiness learning path.
- [Test speed](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/performance/test-speed/index.md): Test your website speed and Internet connection using Cloudflare dashboard tools and third-party services.

## reference

- [Account and domain management best practices](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/best-practices/index.md): Protect your Cloudflare account and domains by decentralizing access, managing billing, and planning for continuity.
- [/cdn-cgi/ endpoint](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/cdn-cgi-endpoint/index.md): Understand the Cloudflare-managed /cdn-cgi/ endpoint added to proxied domains and how various products use it.
- [Cloudflare Ray ID](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/cloudflare-ray-id/index.md): Use Cloudflare Ray IDs to identify and trace individual requests through Security Events, Log Explorer, and server logs.
- [Cloudflare crawlers](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/cloudflare-site-crawling/index.md): Review the user agents and situations in which Cloudflare crawls or makes HTTP requests to your site.
- [Cloudy AI agent (beta)](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/cloudy-ai-agent/index.md): Cloudy is Cloudflare's AI agent that helps you understand and optimize your Cloudflare configurations across multiple products.
- [Connection limits](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/connection-limits/index.md): Review TCP and HTTP connection timeouts between clients, Cloudflare, and origin servers, including keep-alive and request limits.
- [Cryptographic Attestation of Personhood](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/cryptographic-personhood/index.md): Cloudflare's Cryptographic Attestation of Personhood (CAP) lets visitors prove they are human using a hardware key instead of a CAPTCHA.
- [Error responses](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/error-responses/index.md): How Cloudflare formats error responses, including structured JSON and Markdown for agents and API clients, and how they interact with Custom Errors.
- [Glossary](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/glossary/index.md): Review definitions for terms used across Cloudflare products and documentation.
- [Cloudflare and Google Analytics](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/google-analytics/index.md): Understand how Cloudflare's proxy interacts with Google Analytics tracking and how to use GA with Zaraz.
- [Cloudflare HTTP headers](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/http-headers/index.md): Review the HTTP request and response headers that Cloudflare adds or modifies for proxied traffic.
- [Markdown for Agents](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/markdown-for-agents/index.md): Cloudflare's Markdown for Agents converts HTML to Markdown at the edge, allowing AI systems to request content in text/markdown format.
- [SCIM v1 to v2 Migration](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/migration-guides/scim-virtual-groups-migration/index.md): Migrate from SCIM v1 Virtual Groups to Cloudflare's GA SCIM User Groups
- [Network Layers](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/network-layers/index.md): Map Cloudflare products to OSI model layers, from Layer 7 application services to Layer 1 physical connections.
- [Network ports](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/network-ports/index.md): Review the HTTP and HTTPS ports Cloudflare proxies by default and how to enable proxy support for additional ports.
- [Partners](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/partners/index.md): Explore Cloudflare Technology Partner integrations for analytics, networking, Zero Trust, and logging.
- [Cloudflare Cookies](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/policies-compliances/cloudflare-cookies/index.md): Review the cookies Cloudflare sets for load balancing, bot management, challenges, and other product features.
- [Compliance documentation](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/policies-compliances/compliance-docs/index.md): Access and download Cloudflare compliance documents such as PCI, SOC 2, and ISO certifications from the dashboard.
- [Content Security Policies (CSPs)](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/policies-compliances/content-security-policies/index.md): Understand how Cloudflare interacts with Content Security Policies (CSPs) and which headers to update for specific Cloudflare features.
- [Project Cybersafe Schools](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/policies-compliances/cybersafe/index.md): Project Cybersafe Schools provides eligible schools with free Cloudflare Email Security and Gateway products to meet CIPA requirements.
- [Delivering Videos with Cloudflare](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/policies-compliances/delivering-videos-with-cloudflare/index.md): Understand Cloudflare's video delivery policies, resolve Terms of Service redirects, and choose the right paid product for streaming video.
- [Licenses](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/policies-compliances/licenses/index.md): Review the open-source licenses that apply to Cloudflare documentation content and code contributions.
- [Redirects](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/redirects/index.md): Compare Cloudflare redirect options including Single Redirects, Bulk Redirects, Pages Redirects, and Workers.
- [Abuse](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/report-abuse/index.md): Learn how to report DMCA issues, phishing, trademark infringement, malware sites, child exploitation material, and more to Cloudflare's Trust and Safety team.
- [Customer abuse report obligations](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/report-abuse/abuse-report-obligations/index.md): Understand your obligations when Cloudflare forwards an abuse report for a domain associated with your account.
- [Blocked Content](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/report-abuse/blocked-content/index.md): Request removal of Trust and Safety content blocks on your domain.
- [Complaint types](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/report-abuse/complaint-types/index.md): Review the types of abuse complaints you can submit to Cloudflare, including DMCA, phishing, trademark, malware, and child exploitation reports.
- [Providing specific URLs](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/report-abuse/provide-specific-urls/index.md): Learn how to provide specific asset URLs when submitting an abuse report.
- [View and submit reports](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/report-abuse/submit-report/index.md): Submit abuse reports to Cloudflare via the dashboard, public form, or API, and view reports filed against your account.
- [Scans and penetration testing policy](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/scans-penetration/index.md): Understand Cloudflare's policy for conducting vulnerability scans and penetration tests on your own zones and assets.
- [SDK ecosystem support policy](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/sdk-ecosystem-support-policy/index.md): Understand Cloudflare's SDK lifecycle stages, supported language runtimes, and ecosystem support commitments.
- [TCP connections](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/tcp-connections/index.md): Understand how Cloudflare handles TCP connections, keep-alives, and TCP Fast Open between visitors and origin servers.
- [Troubleshooting](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/troubleshooting/index.md): Resolve common error messages and unexpected behavior when setting up your Cloudflare account and domain.
- [Under Attack mode](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/under-attack-mode/index.md): Turn on Cloudflare Under Attack mode to mitigate layer 7 DDoS attacks by challenging suspicious visitors with an interstitial page.

## security

- [Scan for PCI compliance](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/security/pci-scans/index.md): Run PCI compliance scans against your origin server or Cloudflare proxy and interpret the results.
- [Prevent DDoS attacks](https://developers.cloudflare.com/learning-paths/prevent-ddos-attacks/concepts/index.md): Follow the Cloudflare learning path to prevent and mitigate DDoS attacks against your website.
- [Protect your origin server](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/security/protect-your-origin-server/index.md): Secure your origin server with Cloudflare by hiding its IP address, limiting connections, and monitoring health.
- [Recovering from a hacked site](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/security/recovering-from-hacked-site/index.md): Steps to recover your website after a hack and prevent future compromises using Cloudflare security features.
- [Secure your website](https://developers.cloudflare.com/learning-paths/application-security/account-security/index.md): Follow the Cloudflare application security learning path to protect your website and account.
- [Under a DDoS attack?](https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/security/under-ddos-attack/index.md): Learn a few ways to tell if your application is under a DDoS attack.
